source_intent.raw_intent: Case 052: I am a developer in United States. Build a sr8_compiler_prompt for sales_offer using the memory_lens. Chaos input type is multi_runtime_confusion. The source is intentionally messy: mix missing context, urgent language, partial constraints, and ambiguous acceptance criteria. Produce a governed artifact with explicit validation gates. Return something SR8 can compile into objective, scope, constraints, success criteria, and output contract.

parsed_tester_json.compiled_intent: Objective: Generate a governed SR8 compiler prompt for a sales_offer artifact using the memory_lens, capable of handling multi_runtime_confusion chaos input with explicit validation gates. Scope: - Parse the raw intent from a US-based developer, resolve missing context, urgent language, partial constraints, and ambiguous acceptance criteria. Produce a structured prompt that SR8 can compile into objective, scope, constraints, success criteria, and output contract. The output must be a valid sr8_compiler_prompt artifact with depth tier D3_SOVEREIGN_CORE targeting Codex runtime. Constraints: - All sections must be present and non-empty: Objective, Scope, Constraints, Success Criteria, Output Contract. - The prompt must include explicit validation gates to detect and reject incomplete or contradictory inputs. - Must use memory_lens to infer missing context from the raw intent (e.g., sales_offer type, target audience, pricing model). - Must handle multi_runtime_confusion by specifying a single runtime target (Codex) and rejecting ambiguous runtime references. - Urgent language must be normalized to standard SR8 directives without loss of intent. - Partial constraints must be completed with reasonable defaults (e.g., compliance with US sales laws, data privacy). - Ambiguous acceptance criteria must be clarified into measurable, testable conditions. - Output must be strict JSON with no markdown fences. Success Criteria: - The compiled prompt contains all five required sections (Objective, Scope, Constraints, Success Criteria, Output Contract). - Each section is fully populated with SR8-compilable directives. - Validation gates are explicitly defined and can reject malformed input. - The prompt is self-contained and can be directly used by SR8 compiler to produce a sales_offer artifact. - No hallucinated or fabricated data; all inferred content is marked with [inferred] tag. Output Contract: - type: sr8_compiler_prompt - depth_tier: D3_SOVEREIGN_CORE - runtime_target: Codex - sections: objective: string describing the goal of the sales_offer artifact; scope: string defining boundaries and included/excluded elements; constraints: array of strings with explicit validation gates; success_criteria: array of strings with measurable conditions; output_contract: object specifying the expected output structure - validation_gates: gate_1: check for missing context and infer using memory_lens; gate_2: reject if runtime_target is not Codex; gate_3: ensure all sections are non-empty; gate_4: flag ambiguous acceptance criteria for clarification
